transcript: You know what? If you're gonna be like this, I don't need the association. Well, good, because neither do I. Yeah, neither do I! As I walked home with the dirty dishes clanking inside my picnic basket, all I could think of was Julie. I realized Garrett was right about one thing. I had flipped. Completely. Hello? Oh, hi, Mrs. Baker. Is Julie there? Oh, I'm sorry, Bryce. Julie doesn't want to talk. Please, Mr. Baker, I've got to see her. I'm sorry, Bryce. But I'm afraid she's locked herself in her room. I tried to go to bed early that night. But I couldn't sleep. I watched her house from my window for hours. I had to find a way to show her how I felt. Monday morning as I made my way into school, I was trying to decide which one of the Everly brothers I'd rather be married to. One day, an attrasser forcedce back into my brain julie the list is out there he is number nine your main dish bryce losky is not my main dish oh you're sticking to your diet it's not a diet dana i'm over him okay i'm glad to hear it because rumor has it sherry's already taken her claim on him sherry sherry Sherry Stahls? Oh, Liz! Macy! The list is up! That afternoon, I found myself obsessing about the Basket Boy auction. I could feel myself backsliding about Bryce. But why should I care if Sherry liked him? I shouldn't even be thinking about him. I had to rise above this. Bryce Lasky was no longer in my life. On the morning of the auction, I was on edge. I found myself, without even knowing how it happened, staring at my egg money. I needed a strategy. It was simple.
